metadata.title: 聖なる聖なる聖なるかな
metadata.subtitle: Holy, Holy, Holy — Japanese (聖公会讃美歌, 1891)
metadata.lyricist: Reginald Heber (1783–1826) — Japanese translation by the editor of 聖公会讃美歌 (Hugh J. Foss, 1891), at the time titled いときよきかな
metadata.x_pd_basis: Original lyrics by Heber (d. 1826) and tune by Dykes (d. 1876) are PD by > 150 years. Earliest Japanese translation appeared in ヒウ・ゼ・フヲス編『聖公会讃美歌』(1891) no. 96 — > 130 years ago, Meiji 24. Japanese Wikisource hosts the full PD text
metadata.x_translator: Hugh J. Foss (ヒウ・ゼ・フヲス) ed., 聖公会讃美歌 (1891)

chordpro:
{title: 聖なる聖なる聖なるかな}
{subtitle: Holy, Holy, Holy — Japanese, 聖公会讃美歌 (1891) no. 96 「いときよきかな」}
{key: D}
{time: 4/4}
{tempo: 84}
{comment: One of the earliest Japanese hymn translations — Meiji 24 (1891). Original title "いときよきかな"}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[D]聖(きよ)きか[A]な [D]聖(きよ)き[A]かな
kiyoki kana, kiyoki kana
[Bm]聖(きよ)き[F#m]かな主たる [G]全能(ぜんのう)[A]の神[D]
kiyoki kana, shu taru zennou no kami
[D]いとき[G]よ[D]きかな [A]めぐみ[D]ある
ito kiyoki kana, megumi aru
[G]みつのみ[D]くらゐ [A]ひとつなる[D]
mitsu no mikurai, hitotsu naru
[D]かみの[G]み[D]なをば [A]あさま[D]だき
kami no mina wo ba, asa madaki
[G]おきいで[D]ゝこそ [A]ほめまつ[D]れ
okiidete koso, home matsure
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[D]いとき[G]よ[D]きかな [A]とこしへ[D]に
ito kiyoki kana, tokoshie ni
[Bm]ましま[F#m]すかみの [G]まへに[A]ふし[D]
mashimasu kami no, mae ni fushi
[D]ひじりも[G]か[D]むり [A]なげす[D]てゝ
hijiri mo kamuri, nagesutete
[G]みつかひ[D]ともに [A]みなをほ[D]む
mitsukai tomo ni, mina wo homu
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 3}
[D]いとき[G]よ[D]きかな [A]そのみ[D]いづ
ito kiyoki kana, sono mi-izu
[Bm]つみある[F#m]めには [G]みえね[A]ども[D]
tsumi aru me ni wa, mienedomo
[D]みいつく[G]し[D]みの [A]みち〴〵[D]し
mi-itsukushimi no, michimichishi
[G]かみのい[D]さをぞ [A]たくひな[D]き
kami no isawo zo, taguinaki
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 4}
[D]いとき[G]よ[D]きかな [A]つくら[D]れし
ito kiyoki kana, tsukurareshi
[Bm]あめつち[F#m]うみの [G]ものは[A]みな[D]
ametsuchi umi no, mono wa mina
[D]みつのみ[G]く[D]らゐ [A]ひとつ[D]なる
mitsu no mikurai, hitotsu naru
[G]かみのみ[D]なをぞ [A]ほめまつ[D]る
kami no mina wo zo, home matsuru
{end_of_verse}
